turns.text: Q4 was extraordinary. Revenue was $39.3 billion, up 78% year-over-year. Blackwell revenue exceeded $11 billion in Q4 — well above our $8 billion guidance. Demand continues to far outpace supply. We are shipping Blackwell in full production and every customer wants more than we can deliver. For fiscal 2026, we expect data center revenue to grow significantly, driven by Blackwell and our upcoming Rubin architecture.

GAAP gross margin was 73.0% and non-GAAP gross margin was 73.5%, in line with guidance. Full fiscal year 2025 revenue was $130.5 billion, up 114% year-over-year. For fiscal Q1 2026, we expect revenue of $43.0 billion, plus or minus 2%. We expect non-GAAP gross margin to be approximately 70.6% in Q1 as we continue scaling Blackwell production.
Our next question comes from Brent Thill at Jefferies.
Jensen, with DeepSeek's R1 model showing high efficiency at low compute, does that change your view on the need for continued massive data center build-outs from your key customers?

statements.claim: Blackwell revenue exceeded $11 billion in Q4 fiscal 2025, well above the $8 billion guidance.
Blackwell demand continues to far outpace supply and every customer wants more than Nvidia can deliver.
Fiscal Q1 2026 revenue guidance is $43.0 billion, plus or minus 2%.
Non-GAAP gross margin will be approximately 70.6% in fiscal Q1 2026 as Blackwell production scales.
Fiscal year 2025 total revenue was $130.5 billion, up 114% year-over-year.
